primary_accounting_issue: Appréciation du critère SPPI (« prêt basique ») pour un actif financier dont les termes contractuels peuvent modifier le calendrier ou le montant des flux de trésorerie, y compris via un événement déclencheur

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule that IFRS supersedes IAS and selected IFRS 9 because it directly governs SPPI classification and measurement of financial assets, with no higher-precedence competing document in the provided context on the same issue.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: low — IFRS 9 expressly contains the governing SPPI classification requirements and application guidance for contingent and variable cash flow terms.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern whether contractual cash flows with change features or triggers can still be SPPI and how that affects measurement classification.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs clarify boundaries of a basic lending arrangement and constrain the SPPI analysis for modified time value, leverage, prepayment, non-recourse, and contractually linked instruments.

peripheral_authority.reason: These paragraphs concern presentation and disclosure consequences of IFRS 9 classifications rather than identifying the classification approaches themselves.
These paragraphs address hedge accounting under IAS 39 and are topically related to cash flow variability, but they do not govern SPPI classification of financial assets.

treatment_families.family: Financial assets eligible for measurement at amortised cost when the business model is hold to collect and contractual cash flows are SPPI
Financial assets eligible for measurement at fair value through other comprehensive income when the business model is collect and sell and contractual cash flows are SPPI
Residual measurement category for financial assets that do not meet the criteria for amortised cost or FVOCI

approaches.label: Amortised cost
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 treats amortised cost as a distinct measurement model available when the asset both meets SPPI and is held in a hold-to-collect business model.

approaches.label: Fair value through other comprehensive income
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 treats FVOCI as a distinct measurement model available when the asset both meets SPPI and is held in a collect-and-sell business model.

approaches.label: Fair value through profit or loss
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 treats FVTPL as the residual measurement model when the asset does not qualify for amortised cost or FVOCI, including where contractual cash flows are not SPPI.
